{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Azure Developer (Intermediate, Specialist, and Consultant)",
  "description": "Growth in Value Alliance&nbsp; (GV Alliance)&nbsp; Partners&nbsp; is&nbsp; a&nbsp; business&nbsp; advisory&nbsp; and&nbsp; market intelligence services firm. Our objectives are to assist organizations to achieve their growth aspirations by providing market intelligence, strategy blueprint, IT solution delivery expertise and business operation improvement capabilities.

GVA Partners is also a leading name within the recruitment industry. We have built our reputation on over the years by understanding the markets, businesses and individuals within the markets we work with and this has enabled us to deliver seamless and comprehensive recruitment solutions.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Azure Developer (Intermediate, Specialist, and Consultant)

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Qualifications and Experience


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree or equivalent in a relevant discipline (e.g., Computer Science, Engineering, Information Systems, Design, Business, or equivalent).
	Postgraduate degree or relevant professional certifications are an advantage.
	7&ndash;15+ years of relevant professional experience, depending on role level (Junior, Intermediate, Specialist, or Consultant).
	Must have completed NYSC
	Experience working in enterprise-scale digital, IT, or technology environments
	Strong understanding of SDLC and Agile delivery (Scrum / Kanban), and cross?functional collaboration
	Clear communication skills, both written and verbal
	Strong analytical, problem-solving skills, and delivery?focused mindset.


Skills:


	Strong experience with Microsoft Azure services, APIs, and enterprise integrations
	Familiarity with DevOps, CI/CD pipelines, automation tools, and version control
	Understanding of cloud scalability, reliability, and operational performance
	Ability to follow coding, deployment, and documentation standards
	Experience supporting testing, deployment validation, and defect resolution
	Understanding of application monitoring and performance optimization
	Ability to maintain technical documentation, deployment artefacts, and operational guides
	Experience working within cloud governance and release processes
	Ability to implement secure cloud development and deployment practices
	Understanding of access control, monitoring, logging, and compliance standards
	Ability to support multiple cloud initiatives and delivery streams
	Ability to quickly onboard into new cloud platforms and enterprise domains
	Working effectively within multi?disciplinary teams (product, engineering, QA, business)
	Working under defined timelines and adapting to changing priorities and demand.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CVs in PDF to: faitho@gvapartners.com, tisea@gvapartners.com copying contact@gvapartners.com using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",
  "datePosted": "2026-05-22",
  "validThrough": "2026-06-08",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

  },

Azure Developer (Intermediate, Specialist, and Consultant) Job at Growth in Value Alliance




        
    

        
        Growth in Value Alliance  (GV Alliance)  Partners  is  a  business  advisory  and  market intelligence services firm. Our objectives are to assist organizations to achieve their growth aspirations by providing market intelligence, strategy blueprint, IT solution delivery expertise and business operation improvement capabilities.

GVA Partners is also a leading name within the recruitment industry. We have built our reputation on over the years by understanding the markets, businesses and individuals within the markets we work with and this has enabled us to deliver seamless and comprehensive recruitment solutions.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Azure Developer (Intermediate, Specialist, and Consultant)

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Qualifications and Experience


	Bachelor’s degree or equivalent in a relevant discipline (e.g., Computer Science, Engineering, Information Systems, Design, Business, or equivalent).
	Postgraduate degree or relevant professional certifications are an advantage.
	7–15+ years of relevant professional experience, depending on role level (Junior, Intermediate, Specialist, or Consultant).
	Must have completed NYSC
	Experience working in enterprise-scale digital, IT, or technology environments
	Strong understanding of SDLC and Agile delivery (Scrum / Kanban), and cross?functional collaboration
	Clear communication skills, both written and verbal
	Strong analytical, problem-solving skills, and delivery?focused mindset.


Skills:


	Strong experience with Microsoft Azure services, APIs, and enterprise integrations
	Familiarity with DevOps, CI/CD pipelines, automation tools, and version control
	Understanding of cloud scalability, reliability, and operational performance
	Ability to follow coding, deployment, and documentation standards
	Experience supporting testing, deployment validation, and defect resolution
	Understanding of application monitoring and performance optimization
	Ability to maintain technical documentation, deployment artefacts, and operational guides
	Experience working within cloud governance and release processes
	Ability to implement secure cloud development and deployment practices
	Understanding of access control, monitoring, logging, and compliance standards
	Ability to support multiple cloud initiatives and delivery streams
	Ability to quickly onboard into new cloud platforms and enterprise domains
	Working effectively within multi?disciplinary teams (product, engineering, QA, business)
	Working under defined timelines and adapting to changing priorities and demand.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CVs in PDF to: [email protected], [email protected] copying [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Assistant Business Manager",
  "description": "Mama Cass Restaurant is a well-known Nigerian quick service restaurant (QSR) brand that has been around for decades.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Assistant Business Manager

Job Location: Victoria Island, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Overview


	In this role, you will be responsible for supporting the Outlet Manager in running daily operations, ensuring quality service, managing staff, and maintaining outlet standards to drive performance and customer satisfaction.


Key Responsibilities


	Assist in overseeing all aspects of daily operations to ensure an efficient and customer-centric workflow.
	Work closely with the Outlet Manager to achieve sales targets, enhance profitability, and uphold performance standards.
	Monitor team attendance, conduct, and productivity, ensuring alignment with company policies and service expectations.
	Enforce strict adherence to food quality, hygiene, and safety protocols in line with regulatory and brand standards.
	Address and resolve customer concerns promptly and professionally, fostering a positive guest experience.
	Support inventory management processes, including stock audits, ordering, and waste reduction initiatives
	Guide, train, and inspire team members to deliver consistent, high-quality service and operational efficiency.
	Oversee accurate cash handling, daily reconciliation, and ensure compliance with financial procedures.
	Ensure the outlet remains clean, organized, and visually appealing, in line with brand presentation standards.
	Serve as the acting outlet lead in the absence of the Outlet Manager, making sound decisions to maintain business continuity.


Qualifications


	OND / HND / B.Sc Degree in Hospitality, Business Administration, or related fields
	Additional training in food service management is an advantage


Experience:


	3 years of experience in a supervisory or assistant managerial role in a QSR or hospitality setting.


Core Competencies:


	Good leadership and team coordination skills
	Basic inventory and cash handling knowledge
	Strong communication and interpersonal skills
	Customer service focused
	Attention to detail and organizational ability.


Behavioral Traits:


	Proactive and dependable
	Hands-on and supportive
	Positive attitude under pressure
	Strong sense of responsibility
	Flexible and adaptable to shift work.


Salary
N200,000 - N250,000 monthly.&nbsp;&nbsp;

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: begberongbe@mamacassng.com using &quot;Assistant Business Manager&quot; as the subject of the email.

Note: Due to the volume of applications, only qualified candidates will be contacted.",

  },

Assistant Business Manager Job at Mama Cass Restaurant Limited




        
    

        
        Mama Cass Restaurant is a well-known Nigerian quick service restaurant (QSR) brand that has been around for decades.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Assistant Business Manager

Job Location: Victoria Island, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Overview


	In this role, you will be responsible for supporting the Outlet Manager in running daily operations, ensuring quality service, managing staff, and maintaining outlet standards to drive performance and customer satisfaction.


Key Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Assist in overseeing all aspects of daily operations to ensure an efficient and customer-centric workflow.
	Work closely with the Outlet Manager to achieve sales targets, enhance profitability, and uphold performance standards.
	Monitor team attendance, conduct, and productivity, ensuring alignment with company policies and service expectations.
	Enforce strict adherence to food quality, hygiene, and safety protocols in line with regulatory and brand standards.
	Address and resolve customer concerns promptly and professionally, fostering a positive guest experience.
	Support inventory management processes, including stock audits, ordering, and waste reduction initiatives
	Guide, train, and inspire team members to deliver consistent, high-quality service and operational efficiency.
	Oversee accurate cash handling, daily reconciliation, and ensure compliance with financial procedures.
	Ensure the outlet remains clean, organized, and visually appealing, in line with brand presentation standards.
	Serve as the acting outlet lead in the absence of the Outlet Manager, making sound decisions to maintain business continuity.


Qualifications


	OND / HND / B.Sc Degree in Hospitality, Business Administration, or related fields
	Additional training in food service management is an advantage


Experience:


	3 years of experience in a supervisory or assistant managerial role in a QSR or hospitality setting.


Core Competencies:


	Good leadership and team coordination skills
	Basic inventory and cash handling knowledge
	Strong communication and interpersonal skills
	Customer service focused
	Attention to detail and organizational ability.


Behavioral Traits:


	Proactive and dependable
	Hands-on and supportive
	Positive attitude under pressure
	Strong sense of responsibility
	Flexible and adaptable to shift work.


Salary
N200,000 - N250,000 monthly.  

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using "Assistant Business Manager" as the subject of the email.

Note: Due to the volume of applications, only qualified candidates will be contacted.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

  "description": "Westfield Consulting Limited - Our client in the Financial Sector (Microfinance Bank) is recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Debt Recovery officer

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos

Job Summary


	The Debt Recovery Officer is responsible for managing and recovering overdue loans while maintaining a professional relationship with clients.
	The role involves monitoring loan performance, engaging clients with overdue obligations, negotiating repayment plans, and ensuring recovery efforts are carried out in line with company policies.


Job Description


	Monitor the loan portfolio daily to identify non-performing and overdue accounts.
	Liaise with collections officers to review account statuses and recovery strategies.
	Contact defaulting clients via calls, messages, and other approved communication channels to follow up on repayments.
	Send formal demand notices and reminders to clients with overdue obligations.
	Engage clients in constructive discussions to understand reasons for default and explore repayment solutions.
	Negotiate repayment plans and ensure agreed terms are documented and followed.
	Conduct field visits to clients where necessary, in line with company policy.
	Maintain accurate records of all recovery activities and client interactions.
	Escalate difficult or high-risk accounts to management for further action.
	Liaise with relevant stakeholders to track down clients who may have changed location using lawful and professional means.
	Ensure all recovery activities are conducted ethically, professionally, and in compliance with regulatory and organizational standards.


Job Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in Finance, Accounting, Business Administration, or a related field.
	Proven experience in loan recovery, debt collection, or credit management (minimum 1&ndash; 3 years preferred).
	Strong negotiation and communication skills.
	Good understanding of debt recovery procedures.
	Ability to remain calm and professional when dealing with difficult clients.
	Knowledge of relevant financial regulations and ethical collection practices.
	Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
	Ability to maintain accurate records and prepare reports.
	High level of integrity and professionalism.
	Willingness to undertake field visits when required.


Salary
N180,000 - N200,000 per month.&nbsp;

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their current CV to: victor.allu@westfield-consulting.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email

Debt Recovery Officer Job at Westfield Consulting Limited




        
    

        
        Westfield Consulting Limited - Our client in the Financial Sector (Microfinance Bank) is recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Debt Recovery officer

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Debt Recovery Officer is responsible for managing and recovering overdue loans while maintaining a professional relationship with clients.
	The role involves monitoring loan performance, engaging clients with overdue obligations, negotiating repayment plans, and ensuring recovery efforts are carried out in line with company policies.


Job Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Monitor the loan portfolio daily to identify non-performing and overdue accounts.
	Liaise with collections officers to review account statuses and recovery strategies.
	Contact defaulting clients via calls, messages, and other approved communication channels to follow up on repayments.
	Send formal demand notices and reminders to clients with overdue obligations.
	Engage clients in constructive discussions to understand reasons for default and explore repayment solutions.
	Negotiate repayment plans and ensure agreed terms are documented and followed.
	Conduct field visits to clients where necessary, in line with company policy.
	Maintain accurate records of all recovery activities and client interactions.
	Escalate difficult or high-risk accounts to management for further action.
	Liaise with relevant stakeholders to track down clients who may have changed location using lawful and professional means.
	Ensure all recovery activities are conducted ethically, professionally, and in compliance with regulatory and organizational standards.


Job Requirements


	Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Accounting, Business Administration, or a related field.
	Proven experience in loan recovery, debt collection, or credit management (minimum 1– 3 years preferred).
	Strong negotiation and communication skills.
	Good understanding of debt recovery procedures.
	Ability to remain calm and professional when dealing with difficult clients.
	Knowledge of relevant financial regulations and ethical collection practices.
	Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
	Ability to maintain accurate records and prepare reports.
	High level of integrity and professionalism.
	Willingness to undertake field visits when required.


Salary
N180,000 - N200,000 per month. 

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their current CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email

Note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.
